commit.message:
fix: remove lexical from peerDependencies — bundled as dependency

lexical is an internal implementation detail. Users never touch the
Lexical API directly (no public types, no exported Lexical instances).
Making it a peer was wrong — it required users to install an extra
package they never use.

- Remove peerDependencies.lexical from @lexify/core
- Remove peerDependencies.lexical from @lexify/plugins
- Update install instructions in all READMEs and docs

Users now only need:
  pnpm add @lexify/core

minor-fixes: infer update channel from binary version, block PR builds (#7011)

Two fixes for azd update:

1. Channel auto-detection: LoadUpdateConfig() defaults to ChannelStable
when no config exists, causing daily builds to check the wrong update
source and display 'stable channel'. Add inferChannelFromVersion() that
detects 'daily.' in the binary version string. Both azd update and the
background auto-update check now use the correct channel by default.
Explicit config still takes precedence.

2. Block PR builds: Replace IsDevVersion() with IsNonProdVersion() guard
in update.go and main.go so both dev builds (0.0.0-dev.0) and PR builds
(1.24.0-beta.1-pr.XXXXX) are blocked from self-updating.

This gives the GITHUB_TOKEN permission to upload release assets, which is required when workflows attach files to releases.

* Change permissions from read to write for contents

Made-with: Cursor

* milady: fix Electron build CI — add tar for node-pre-gyp, use @electron/asar in crash dump

Made-with: Cursor

* chore: standardize branding ElizaOS → elizaOS across project

Made-with: Cursor

* milady: remove tar override, add tar@^6.1.11 to root deps

The tar@^7.5.7 override forced all tar resolutions to 7.x, but
@mapbox/node-pre-gyp requires tar@^6.1.11. electron-builder's
TraversalNodeModulesCollector does a strict semver.satisfies() check
and rejects 7.x for a ^6.1.11 range, causing the "Production
dependency tar not found" build failure.

Made-with: Cursor

* milady: align electron pty-manager to 1.9.5 to match root override

The electron app pinned pty-manager@1.9.0 but the root override
forces 1.9.5. electron-builder's semver.satisfies("1.9.5", "1.9.0")
returns false for an exact pin, causing "Production dependency
pty-manager not found for package Milady".

Made-with: Cursor

* milady: add setup-node 22 to test jobs with native compilation

The app-startup-e2e, electron-ui-e2e, and electron-packaged-dmg-e2e
jobs relied on the runner's system Node (20.20.0). @discordjs/opus
has no prebuilt binary for Node 20's ABI, and node-gyp failed to
fetch Node 20 headers. Adding setup-node@v4 with node 22 aligns CI
with the project's engines requirement and provides correct ABI
binaries.

Made-with: Cursor

commit.message:
feat(quality): anti-slop + anti-cheat rules — adversarial.py + skills

  skip decorators, and assert True to produce green CIs without fixing bugs.
  Two-layer enforcement: adversarial.py L0 (automatic rejection) + skills
  (upstream guidance so the agent never generates slop in the first place).

adversarial.py — NEW pattern lists:
  _TEST_CHEAT_PATTERNS (18 patterns, score +5 each = hard reject at threshold 5):
    - pytest.mark.skip/skipif, xit(), xtest(), xdescribe()
    - test.skip(), it.skip(), describe.skip()
    - assert True, assert 1 (trivially-passing assertions)
    - toBeTruthy() as sole assertion
    - Empty test body: def test_X(): pass / def test_X(): ...
    - Empty Jest body: it('x', () => {})
    - except Exception: pass inside test code
    - --cov-fail-under=0, fail_under=0 (coverage bypass)
    - TEST_MODE env bypass injected into production code

  _CODE_SLOP_PATTERNS (9 patterns, score +2 each, capped at 3 per file):
    - @ts-ignore, @ts-nocheck (TypeScript error suppression)
    - eslint-disable blocks
    - # type: ignore (mypy suppression)
    - except Exception: pass (bare error swallowing)
    - -webkit- vendor prefixes for border-radius/box-shadow/transition/transform
    - -moz- vendor prefixes (same)

  check_l0(): new detection blocks after mock/stub scan:
    - is_test_file: checks _TEST_CHEAT_PATTERNS
    - is_code_file (.py/.ts/.tsx/.js/.jsx/.css/.scss/.less): checks _CODE_SLOP_PATTERNS

  SCOPE docstring: updated to reflect new coverage

skills/clean-code.md (NEW):
  7 rules covering all languages:
    1. Never cheat tests (full list with examples)
    2. No fake/placeholder data (lorem ipsum, test@test.com, Math.random() IDs)
    3. No type/lint suppression (TS, Python — with correct alternatives)
    5. No dead code (unused imports, commented blocks, debug prints)
    6. No magic values (named constants + examples)
    7. No fallback slop for >95% support CSS features
  L1 prompt section: structured check instructions for the semantic LLM reviewer

skills/frontend-design.md:
  Added CSS clean code rules to anti-patterns section:
    no hardcoded hex, no spacing inline, no vendor prefix without caniuse check,
    no fallback for >95% support, no inline style=, no z-index:9999

fix: address critical and major review findings for attachment feature

🔴 CRITICAL: Fix body parser ordering — the route-level json({ limit: '20mb' })
middleware on message routes was dead code because the global
express.json({ limit: '1mb' }) ran first and rejected large payloads.
Fix: raise global limit to 10mb and remove dead per-route json middleware.

🟠 MAJOR: Add attachment schema security hardening:
- Restrict mimeType to image/png, image/jpeg, image/gif, image/webp
  (blocks SVG XSS attacks)
- Add .max(14_000_000) to data field (~10MB binary after base64 encoding)

🟠 MAJOR: Fix error handling in LeadDashboard.tsx — clearAttachments()
now only runs on successful send (inside try/catch, after resp.ok check)

🟠 MAJOR: Add supportsImages check in buildContentBlocks() — when the
agent doesn't support images, image attachments are converted to text
descriptions instead of being sent as image content blocks.

commit.message:
[Agent] feat(cheats): edit support, tvOS type mapping, multi-line input & legacy UIKit removal (#2508)

* feat(cheats): edit support, tvOS type mapping, multi-line input, remove legacy UIKit

- Add iOSEditCheatView and TVOSEditCheatView for in-place cheat editing
  (swipe-left on iOS, pencil button on tvOS) with Realm write on save
- Wire tvOS cheat database import to map deviceName to CheatCodeTypes
- Add CheatCodeValidator.supportsMultiLine() and use TextEditor for
  Gecko/GameShark formats in Add and Edit views on both platforms
- Remove legacy UIKit storyboard path; SwiftUI is now the only path
- Delete 6 legacy UIKit cheat files (UITableViewController, stubs, etc.)
- Move CheatsCompletion typealias to CheatsResult.swift

Closes #2503 (must-have items)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(cheats): address Copilot review issues in cheat UI

- Replace deprecated `.foregroundColor` with `.foregroundStyle` throughout
  TVOSCheatsView.swift (50+ occurrences)
- Fix Realm predicate string interpolation: use NSPredicate(format:) instead
  of string interpolation in loadCheats() for iOS and tvOS views
- Remove inline `try!` for regex; move to pre-compiled static constants with
  swiftlint suppressions matching project patterns (CheatCodeValidator style)
- Fix implicitly unwrapped optional `var cheatsState: PVCheats!` → optional
- Fix force unwraps `code.file!.url!` → safe optional chaining with guard
- Replace `NSLog`/`print` with `DLOG`/`ELOG` logging macros
- Fix `saveCheat()` calling `dismiss()` even when Realm write fails in both
  iOSEditCheatView and TVOSEditCheatView
- Remove redundant `NoCheatCompletion` typealias (identical to CheatsCompletion)
- Extend `supportsMultiLine` to include "action replay" and "code breaker"
  formats which also use multi-line linesMatch validation
- Remove trailing semicolons and unnecessary parentheses in conditionals

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(cheats): address remaining Copilot review issues

- Replace contentModificationDate! force unwrap with safe ?? Date.distantPast
  fallback in recoverCheatCodes() to prevent crash when modification date
  is unavailable in file resource values
- Remove redundant `return` statements at the end of else blocks in
  setCheatState() and cheatsViewControllerUpdateState() — both were
  the last statement in the function so the explicit return was dead code

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(cheats): address Copilot review issues

- Fix enabled:false hardcoded bug in setCheatState(): use the enabled
  parameter when creating the PVCheats Realm object so cheats are
  persisted with the correct enabled state instead of always disabled
- Replace deprecated NavigationView with NavigationStack in
  TVOSCheatsView (tvOS 16+) and replace deprecated
  .navigationBarHidden(true) with .toolbar(.hidden, for: .navigationBar)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(cheats): use codeType field in edit views, remove -~- encoding

iOSEditCheatView and TVOSEditCheatView were still reading code type
by splitting cheat.type on the legacy "-~-" separator and re-encoding
it on save, bypassing the dedicated codeType field added in schema v24.

- In init(): read cheat.codeType directly; fall back to parsing
  the -~- separator only for pre-migration records where codeType=""
- In saveCheat(): write cheat.type = name and cheat.codeType = selectedType
  separately, eliminating the -~- join that conflicts with schema v24

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(cheats): address Copilot review issues

- Eliminate duplicate trimmingCharacters(in:) calls in saveCheat() of
  iOSAddCheatView, iOSEditCheatView, and TVOSEditCheatView — store the
  trimmed value in a local variable before the conditional
- Rename `type` loop parameter to `codeType` in TVOSAddCheatView and
  TVOSEditCheatView ForEach closures to avoid shadowing the builtin type(of:)
- Rename shadowed `cheat` variables in recoverCheatCodes() to `cheatInfo`
  and `realmCheat` to eliminate variable shadowing warnings

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(serializer): add async storeMetadata overload, fix isDirectory bug

- Add async storeMetadata overload to LibrarySerializer for structured
  concurrency support alongside the existing callback-based version
- Fix isDirectory: true -> false for JSON sidecar path in storeMetadata
  callback version (the path refers to a file, not a directory)
- Update PVEmulatorViewController+Cheats to use the new async overload,
  eliminating the nested Task wrapping storeMetadata completion callback
